rspStmt.AuthEnty: Research on Open Educational Resources for Development (ROER4D)

serStmt.serInfo: Research on Open Educational Resources for Development. Faculty Perceptions of OER in India. (ROER4D Sub-project 3) [dataset]. Version 1. Cape Town: ROER4D [producer], 2015. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2016. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/wz4y-rz39

verStmt.version: v1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: Version 1 of the dataset was deposited with DataFirst on 23 August 2016.

stdyInfo.abstract: The study aims to map the current preconceptions and behavioural determinants that influence academics' engagement with Open Educational Resources (OER) in India. The key objectives of this research were to examine teachers' attitudes towards OER in selected Indian universities, establish teacher perspectives on OER use in order to establish the uptake of OER in India, identify barriers to the creation and use of OER in India, identify the factors motivating teachers' uptake of OER in India, analyse teachers' perceptions of quality of OER in India, and investigate relationships amongst teachers' attitudes, motivation, perceptions of quality and barriers to the use of OER. The project utilised a mixed methods approach consisting of a survey and unstructured interviews conducted with academics from four Indian universities. The resultant dataset includes a qualitative component (interview transcripts) and a quantitative component (survey data).

sumDscr.geogCover: The interviews and survey were conducted at four institutions in India and are not representative of the country as a whole.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The data covers selected universities in India and not the country as a whole.
sumDscr.universe: The study engaged academics in the four institutions in India involved in the study.

stdyInfo.notes: Topics covered are teachers' attitudes towards OER, teachers' perceptions of quality of OER, and barriers to the use of OER amongst teachers in India.

dataColl.collSitu: The interviews were conducted with 27 instructors, and comprised between 49-55 questions. The survey component consisted of a survey created with an online tool and distributed via email and during workshop sessions. The survey gathered 117 usable responses.

useStmt.citReq: Research on Open Educational Resources for Development. Perceptions of Open Educational Resources in India. (ROER4D Sub-project 3) [dataset]. Version 1. Cape Town: ROER4D [producer], 2015. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2016. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/wz4y-rz39
useStmt.conditions: Public use files, available to all

var.name: Respondent_code
var.labl: Respondent code

var.name: ResearchSite
var.labl: Research site

var.name: Gender
var.labl: Gender

var.name: Age
var.labl: Age

var.labl: Designation

var.name: Discipline
var.labl: Discipline

var.name: Highest_qualification
var.labl: Highest educational qualification

var.name: Teaching_Experience
var.labl: Teaching experience

var.name: Nature_of_job
var.labl: Nature of job

var.name: Language_1
var.labl: Common language 1

var.name: Language_2
var.labl: Common language 2

var.name: Language_3
var.labl: Common language 3

var.name: Institution
var.labl: Institution

var.name: Status_of_institution
var.labl: Status of institution

var.name: Nature_of_Institution
var.labl: Nature of institution

var.name: No_of_students
var.labl: No of students

var.name: Previous_OER_use
var.labl: Used OER previously

var.name: Previous_OER_contribution
var.labl: Contributed OER previously

var.name: ATOER6
var.labl: Have knowledge of Intellectual Property Rights to understand OER

var.name: ATOER7
var.labl: Sharing educational resources improves my professional respect

var.name: ATOER8
var.labl: It gives pleasure if someone adopts/adapts my educational resources

var.name: ATOER9
var.labl: Sharing helps me to get feedback

var.name: ATOER10
var.labl: Sharing enhances my personal and organizational reputation

var.name: ATOER11
var.labl: Sharing of educational resources increases my profile amongst peers

var.name: ATOER12
var.labl: OER increases my network and sphere of influence

var.name: ATOER13
var.labl: It is my responsibility to share all educational resources created by me

var.name: ATOER15
var.labl: OER improves my chance of recognition at a global level

var.name: ATOER16
var.labl: Sharing educational materials as OER will encourage others to do so well

var.name: ATOER17
var.labl: Sharing enhances my confidence as I see myself in part of larger community

var.name: ATOER18
var.labl: When others use my OER, it improves my sense of achievement

var.name: ATOER19
var.labl: OER helps to disseminate my ideas

var.name: ATOER23
var.labl: OER promotes collaboration and consortia

var.name: ATOER24
var.labl: I am efficient in ICT skilld to adopt and use OER

var.name: ATOER25
var.labl: I adopt OER for my teaching as they fullfil academic requirements of my students

var.name: ATOER26
var.labl: My own competencies and knowledge towards OER help me to participate or adopt OE

var.name: Motivation1
var.labl: I believe that OER are good for people as it improves their learning.

var.name: Motivation2
var.labl: I try to contribute to OER to give back to society

var.name: Motivation3
var.labl: I like to be involved in peer production of OER

var.name: Motivation4
var.labl: Through OER, I can reach disadvantaged communities

var.name: Motivation5
var.labl: OER will help developing countries increase access to education

var.name: Motivation6
var.labl: Sharing knowledge is a basic academic value

var.name: Motivation7
var.labl: OER caters to innate desire to learn, improve and progress

var.name: Motivation8
var.labl: OER gives me opportunities to learn new things

var.name: Motivation9
var.labl: OER saves my time

var.name: Motivation10
var.labl: OER is less expensive

var.name: Motivation11
var.labl: OER provides access to best materials and teachers

var.name: Motivation12
var.labl: OER increases my self-confidence

var.name: Motivation13
var.labl: Involvement in OER will give me recognition

var.name: Motivation14
var.labl: OER improves professional image

var.name: Motivation15
var.labl: Receiving appropriate credit (such as API scores) will help me uptake OER

var.name: Motivation16
var.labl: I like receiving comments and feedbacks from experts and seniors on OER created

var.name: Motivation17
var.labl: OER provides us opportunities for establishing new partnerships

var.name: Motivation18
var.labl: Technology associated with OER is easy

var.name: Motivation19
var.labl: I know about my Intellectual Property Rights under Creative Commons licenses

var.name: Quality1
var.labl: OER saves time of the teachers

var.name: Quality2
var.labl: OERs brings down the cost of learning materials

var.name: Quality3
var.labl: OERs are free resources available in open license

var.name: Quality4
var.labl: I do not need permission to re-use OER

var.name: Quality5
var.labl: I often use OER which fulfill the pedagogical need of teaching

var.name: Quality6
var.labl: If OERs are appropriate in their content I prefer to use them

var.name: Quality7
var.labl: I prefer to use OER from trustworthy sources

var.name: Quality8
var.labl: Open license of OER enables continuous quality improvement

var.name: Quality9
var.labl: I use trustworthy OER from reputed institutions

var.name: Quality10
var.labl: Lack of peer review of OERs make them susceptible to poor quality

var.name: Quality11
var.labl: OERs assist the developing countries to have quality materials

var.name: Quality12
var.labl: Quality of OER is questionable

var.name: Quality13
var.labl: OER needs localization

var.name: barrier1
var.labl: Lack of understanding of IP licenses, Copyrights and Creative Commons licenses

var.name: barrier1_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier1

var.name: barrier2
var.labl: Lack of knowledge for using OER in my teaching and learning process

var.name: barrier2_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier2

var.name: barrier3
var.labl: Lack of recognition and rewards system for developing OER

var.name: barrier3_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier3

var.name: barrier4
var.labl: Lack of financial resources by institution to invest in OER

var.name: barrier4_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier4

var.name: barrier5
var.labl: Lack of technological support to resolve my problems

var.name: barrier5_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier5

var.name: barrier6
var.labl: In-ability to find existing OER on topics of my interest

var.name: barrier6_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier6

var.name: barrier7
var.labl: Lack of confidence about the quality of my work

var.name: barrier7_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier7

var.name: barrier8
var.labl: Incompatibility of OER to my university Learning Management System (LMS)

var.name: barrier8_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier8

var.name: barrier9
var.labl: Difficulty to remix OER for specific users

var.name: barrier9_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier9

var.name: barrier10
var.labl: Unavailability of OER in native language

var.name: barrier10_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier10

var.name: barrier11
var.labl: Lack of ICT skills required to create OER

var.name: barrier11_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier11

var.name: barrier12
var.labl: Lack of Institutional policy on OER

var.name: barrier12_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier12

var.name: barrier13
var.labl: Non-user friendly OER platforms

var.name: barrier13_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier13

var.name: barrier14
var.labl: Poor technical infrastructure

var.name: barrier14_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier14

var.name: barrier15
var.labl: Difficulty in collaboration

var.name: barrier15_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier15

var.name: barrier16
var.labl: Inadequate bandwidth

var.name: barrier16_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier16

var.name: barrier17
var.labl: Current workload

var.name: barrier17_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier17

var.name: barrier18
var.labl: Lack of time

var.name: barrier18_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier18

var.name: barrier19
var.labl: Any other

var.name: barrier19_rank
var.labl: Rank of barrier19
